title: NZ Holiday Parks Family Guide — TOP 10 vs Freedom Camping
meta_desc: Discover the best NZ holiday parks and freedom camping options for your family with our complete guide
intro: With over 300 holiday parks in New Zealand, choosing the right one for your family can be overwhelming. TOP 10 holiday parks offer a range of facilities, but freedom camping can be a more budget-friendly option. In this guide, we'll break down the costs, facilities, and best parks by region to help you make an informed decision.

sections.heading: TOP 10 Holiday Parks vs Freedom Camping
sections.body: TOP 10 holiday parks offer a wide range of facilities, including kitchens, laundry, and play areas, with prices starting from around $40 per night for a powered site. Freedom camping, on the other hand, can be as low as $10 per night, but often requires a self-contained vehicle and has limited amenities.

sections.heading: Best Parks by Region
sections.body: Some of the best TOP 10 holiday parks in New Zealand include Auckland's Takapuna Beach Holiday Park, Rotorua's Holdens Bay Holiday Park, and Queenstown's Creeksyde Holiday Park. For freedom camping, consider the DOC sites in the South Island, such as those in Abel Tasman National Park or Mount Aspiring National Park.

sections.heading: Booking Tips
list: Book in advance, especially during peak season
Check the park's website for deals and discounts
Consider a loyalty program, such as TOP 10's Explorer programme
Read reviews and check the park's ratings before booking

sections.heading: Getting There
sections.body: New Zealand has a well-developed road network, making it easy to get to most holiday parks. For example, it's around a 3-hour drive from Auckland to Rotorua, and around a 6-hour drive from Christchurch to Queenstown. You can also use public transport, such as buses and trains, or rent a campervan for the ultimate road trip experience.

cta.text: Use our NZ Family Travel Budget Calculator to plan your trip
Check out our campervan guide for more information on renting a campervan

faq.question: What is the difference between a TOP 10 holiday park and a regular holiday park?
faq.answer: TOP 10 is a quality franchise — members must meet standards for facilities, cleanliness, and service. TOP 10 parks tend to have better playgrounds, TV rooms, jumping pillows, and communal kitchens. Regular holiday parks can be just as good (or better) at lower prices but vary more in quality.

faq.question: How much does a NZ holiday park cost per night for a family?
faq.answer: Powered sites (electricity + water hookup): $40–$75/night for 2 adults + 2 kids. Cabins: $80–$140/night. Motel units: $130–$220/night. TOP 10 parks charge a premium of $10–$15 above standard parks. Prices increase 30–50% over Christmas/New Year and peak school holidays.

faq.question: Can you stay at NZ holiday parks without a campervan?
faq.answer: Yes — all NZ holiday parks have cabins, tourist flats, and motel units for tent campers and those without campervans. Budget cabins (bring your own bedding) start from $60–$80/night. Most parks also have tent sites from $20–$40/night per adult. All have shared kitchens, bathrooms, and facilities.
